content.full_text:
January 15, 1980

Robert P. Houston  
Kaiser Steel Corporation  
California Government Relations Office  
1500 Seventh Street, Ste. 13M  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Houston:

Thank you for your letter of December 18, 1979, regarding whether you must report rides to work which you provide to Mr. Nelson Whitely who is employed in the Senate Mail Room.

As a lobbyist, the Act requires you to report payments to influence legislative or administrative action, including payments to legislative officials. Government Code Section 82038 defines "legislative official" as "any employee or consultant of the Legislature whose duties are not solely secretarial, clerical or manual." Mr. Whitely's duties, as described to me, consist solely of sorting mail and framing bills, and do not entail the making of government decisions. Therefore, his duties appear to be covered in the exception in Section 82038. This conclusion is bolstered by the fact that Mr. Whitely is not covered by the Legislature's conflict of interest code which is intended to cover all members of the legislative staff who make or participate in the making of governmental decisions.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Thomas M. Sobel  
Chief  
Legal Division
